summary: The plan is well-aligned with the existing architecture. The codebase already has the WebGL renderer, render loop, and geometry system interfaces in place — the migration is completing stubbed implementations rather than replacing a working Canvas 2D backend. The plan correctly identifies all files that need changes and proposes minimal, focused modifications.

architectural_impact.reasoning: Changes are confined to the visual subsystem (src/visual/) with no impact on audio, input, seed, UI, or server modules. The GeometrySystem interface gains an optional cleanup() method, which is additive and non-breaking. No new external dependencies are introduced — all Three.js primitives (Points, Line, BufferGeometry) are already available via the existing three dependency.

interface_changes.change: Add optional cleanup() method to GeometrySystem interface
interface_changes.notes: Optional method preserves backward compatibility with existing implementations and tests

interface_changes.change: Return lights alongside mesh for proper cleanup
interface_changes.notes: Return type changes from { mesh } to { mesh, ambient, directional }; only consumed in main.ts and renderLoop.ts

risks.description: Three.js objects (Points, Line, BufferGeometry) require WebGL context which jsdom does not provide. Tests will need mock strategies or simplified assertions.
risks.mitigation: The existing test suite already mocks Three.js Scene via vi.mock('three') — extend the same pattern for Points, Line, and BufferGeometry. Verify buffer attribute lengths and scene.add() calls rather than rendered output.

risks.description: Per-frame buffer updates for up to 600 particles (3 floats × 600 = 1800 floats for positions) is well within WebGL budget. LineBasicMaterial with ~128 vertices per wave × 20 waves is similarly lightweight.
risks.mitigation: Plan already notes dirty-range optimization as future possibility. Current scale is not a concern.

risks.description: Mode switching requires disposing Three.js geometries and materials to avoid GPU memory leaks. If cleanup() is not called or is incomplete, leaked resources accumulate over session lifetime.
risks.mitigation: Step 4 correctly addresses this by adding cleanup() to GeometrySystem and calling it in modeManager before switching. Plan should ensure dispose() is called on both geometry and material objects.

risks.description: Transition from placeholder wireframe icosahedron to particle/wave systems changes the visible output significantly. No visual regression testing framework exists.
risks.mitigation: This is expected and desired — the whole point of the ticket. Manual verification during development is sufficient.

plan_gaps.description: Step 3 mentions removing placeholder lights but the plan should clarify whether the ambient light added in Step 5 is added before or after placeholder removal to avoid a flash of unlit frames.
plan_gaps.recommendation: Add the persistent ambient light to scene.ts (Step 5) before implementing placeholder removal (Step 3), or reorder steps 5 and 3.

plan_gaps.description: The plan does not address the camera position relative to the new geometry. The camera is at z=5 looking at origin. Particle positions are currently normalized 0-1 — they need to be mapped to 3D world coordinates visible from the camera.
plan_gaps.recommendation: Step 1 details should specify the coordinate mapping: e.g., particles span [-aspect, aspect] in X/Y and [-1, 1] in Z, centered at origin within the camera frustum.

plan_gaps.description: Window resize handling in main.ts updates renderer and camera but does not notify geometry systems. If wave vertex positions depend on aspect ratio, they need recomputation on resize.
plan_gaps.recommendation: FrameState already includes width/height — wave draw() can recompute positions each frame from these values, making resize handling implicit.

dependency_analysis.notes: US-029 (quality tiers), US-017 (audio analysis), US-013 (session seed) are all implemented. The geometry system stubs, quality config, audio pipeline, and render loop infrastructure are all in place.

alignment_with_project_principles.privacy: no_impact — no new data collection or storage, no changes to privacy-sensitive modules
alignment_with_project_principles.performance: positive — Three.js buffer-based rendering is more GPU-efficient than Canvas 2D for particle/wave systems; quality tier caps already in place
alignment_with_project_principles.graceful_degradation: maintained — render loop continues functioning with empty draw() during async init; placeholder provides visual feedback until geometry systems are ready
alignment_with_project_principles.reduced_motion: maintained — motionAmplitude scaling is already in FrameState.params and plan applies it to all movement

step_ordering_assessment.notes: Steps 1-2 (implement systems) are independent and could be parallelized. Step 3 (placeholder cleanup) depends on 1-2 being complete. Step 4 (mode switch cleanup) depends on both systems having cleanup(). Step 5 (lighting) should ideally be done before step 3 to avoid unlit frames. Step 6 (verification) is correctly last.
